annotations.label: Torus

anchor.snippet: a torus ( pl. : tori or toruses ) is a surface of revolution generated by revolving a circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of a surface of revolution or the embedded donut surface in ℝ³.

annotations.label: Ring torus

anchor.snippet: the surface has a ring shape and is called a torus of revolution

annotations.note: Mathlib has no surface-of-revolution torus and so no ring-torus subclass.

annotations.label: Horn torus

anchor.snippet: If the axis of revolution is tangent to the circle, the surface is a horn torus

annotations.note: The horn-torus degeneration of a surface of revolution is not formalized.

annotations.label: Spindle torus

anchor.snippet: If the axis of revolution passes twice through the circle, the surface is a spindle torus

annotations.note: The self-intersecting spindle torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Degenerate torus

anchor.snippet: If the axis of revolution passes through the center of the circle, the surface is a degenerate torus

annotations.note: The degenerate (sphere-of-revolution-like) case is not formalized.

annotations.label: Toroid

anchor.snippet: If the revolved curve is not a circle, the surface is called a toroid

annotations.note: No general surface-of-revolution / toroid construction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Solid torus

anchor.snippet: A solid torus is a torus plus the volume inside the torus

annotations.note: Grep for `solidTorus`/`SolidTorus` returns nothing in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ring torus is homeomorphic to S¹ × S¹

anchor.snippet: a ring torus is homeomorphic to the Cartesian product of two circles

annotations.note: Without an embedded ring-torus definition Mathlib cannot state this homeomorphism.

annotations.label: Clifford torus (embedding in 4-space)

anchor.snippet: This produces a geometric object called the Clifford torus

annotations.note: No `CliffordTorus` definition exists; only the unrelated `CliffordAlgebra` shares the name.

annotations.label: Torus from a rectangular strip

anchor.snippet: An example of a torus can be constructed by taking a rectangular strip of flexible material

annotations.note: Informal construction not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parametrization of the torus of revolution

anchor.snippet: A torus of revolution in 3-space can be parametrized as:

annotations.note: `torusMap` is a different (n-fold circle in ℂⁿ) parametrization, not the 3D donut parametrization.

annotations.label: Aspect ratio of a torus

anchor.snippet: The ratio R / r is called the aspect ratio of the torus

annotations.note: No 3D torus geometry, hence no aspect-ratio notion.

annotations.label: Implicit Cartesian equation

anchor.snippet: An implicit equation in Cartesian coordinates for a torus radially symmetric about the z- axis is

annotations.note: No Cartesian-equation statement for the 3-space torus surface in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quartic equation for the torus

anchor.snippet: Algebraically eliminating the square root gives a quartic equation

annotations.note: Quartic implicit equation for the embedded torus is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification by aspect ratio

anchor.snippet: The three classes of standard tori correspond to the three possible aspect ratios between R and r

annotations.note: Tied to the embedded R/r classification, which Mathlib does not define.

annotations.label: Volume and surface area via Pappus

anchor.snippet: The volume of this solid torus and the surface area of its torus are easily computed using Pappus's centroid theorem

annotations.note: Pappus's centroid theorem is not in Mathlib (no `Pappus` grep hits).

annotations.label: Same formulae as the unrolled cylinder

anchor.snippet: These formulae are the same as for a cylinder of length 2π R and radius r

annotations.note: Depends on volume/area formulas above which are absent.

annotations.label: Surface area and volume in terms of p and q

anchor.snippet: Expressing the surface area and the volume by the distance p of an outermost point on the surface of the torus to the center

annotations.note: Variant of the same area/volume formulas; not formalized.

annotations.label: Toroidal and poloidal directions

anchor.snippet: but is known as the "toroidal" direction

annotations.note: Toroidal/poloidal coordinate naming is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Partial angular velocity fields

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: The partial angular velocity fields are given as

annotations.note: Specific to the embedded ring torus; not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Transpose of the Jacobian matrix

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: The transpose of the Jacobian matrix

annotations.note: No ring-torus parametrization, hence no Jacobian computation.

annotations.label: Jacobian determinant of the ring torus

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: can then be computed as the Euclidean norm (magnitude) of the cross product

annotations.note: Concrete ring-torus Jacobian determinant is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laplace operator on the ring torus

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: the Laplace operator for the ring torus, with the same variable ranges as for the integrals above, is given by the expression below

annotations.note: The explicit Laplace–Beltrami expression on the embedded ring torus is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Metric tensor of the ring torus

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: The metric tensor can be calculated as

annotations.note: Metric tensor of the embedded ring torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: √det(g) equals det of Jacobi matrix

anchor.section: Differential geometry for the ring torus
anchor.snippet: the square root of the determinant of the metric tensor is equal to the determinant of the Jacobi matrix

annotations.note: Embedded-ring-torus identity; not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Topological torus as product of two circles

anchor.snippet: a torus is a closed surface defined as the product of two circles

annotations.note: `UnitAddTorus d = d → UnitAddCircle` realises the topological torus as a product of circles (the 2-torus is the `d = Fin 2` case).

annotations.label: S³ as nested family of tori (Hopf bundle)

anchor.snippet: is filled out by a family of nested tori in this manner

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks the Hopf fibration / nested-tori description of S³.

annotations.label: Surface is homeomorphic to the topological torus

anchor.snippet: given the relative topology from R 3 , is homeomorphic to a topological torus as long as it does not intersect its own axis

annotations.note: Requires the embedded ring-torus that Mathlib does not define.

annotations.label: Torus as quotient of the Cartesian plane

anchor.snippet: The torus can also be described as a quotient of the Cartesian plane under the identifications

annotations.note: `UnitAddTorus (Fin 2)` is `(ℝ/ℤ) × (ℝ/ℤ)`, the plane quotient by the integer lattice.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of the torus

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of the torus is just the direct product of the fundamental group of the circle with itself

annotations.note: The product isomorphism of fundamental groupoids exists, but π₁(S¹) = ℤ and the resulting π₁(T²) ≃ ℤ² are not assembled as a named theorem.

annotations.label: Higher homotopy groups are trivial

anchor.snippet: Its higher homotopy groups are all trivial

annotations.note: `HomotopyGroup` exists but no computation πₙ(T²)=0 for n≥2 is stated.

annotations.label: Homology groups of the torus

anchor.snippet: The torus has homology groups

annotations.note: Singular/cellular homology of the torus is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First homology isomorphic to fundamental group

anchor.snippet: the first homology group of the torus is isomorphic to its fundamental group

annotations.note: Hurewicz/abelianisation comparison for the torus is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cohomology groups isomorphic to homology groups

anchor.snippet: The cohomology groups with integer coefficients are isomorphic to the homology ones

annotations.note: No torus cohomology computation in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Punctured torus turned inside out yields another torus

anchor.snippet: If a torus is punctured and turned inside out then another torus results

annotations.note: Informal topological observation, not formalized.

annotations.label: 2-torus is a twofold branched cover of the 2-sphere

anchor.snippet: The 2-torus is a twofold branched cover of the 2-sphere, with four ramification points

annotations.note: Branched covers and ramification of T² over S² are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every conformal structure arises as such a cover

anchor.snippet: Every conformal structure on the 2-torus can be represented as such a two-sheeted cover of the 2-sphere

annotations.note: Conformal structures on the 2-torus are not formalized.

annotations.label: Weierstrass points

anchor.snippet: The points on the torus corresponding to the ramification points are the Weierstrass points

annotations.note: No Weierstrass-points definition appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conformal type determined by cross-ratio

anchor.snippet: the conformal type of the torus is determined by the cross-ratio of the four points

annotations.note: No moduli/cross-ratio statement for tori in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n-dimensional torus

anchor.snippet: the n -dimensional torus is topologically equivalent to the product of n circles

annotations.note: `UnitAddTorus d := d → UnitAddCircle` is exactly the indexed product of circles.

annotations.label: n-torus as quotient Rⁿ/Zⁿ

anchor.snippet: the n -torus is R n modulo the action of the integer lattice Z n

annotations.note: Each factor `UnitAddCircle = AddCircle (1:ℝ) = ℝ⧸ℤ`, so `UnitAddTorus d` is `(d→ℝ)/(d→ℤ)`.

annotations.label: n-torus is a compact manifold and a compact abelian Lie group

anchor.snippet: An n -torus in this sense is an example of an n- dimensional compact manifold

annotations.note: Compactness of each `AddCircle p` is shown (so the product is compact), but Lie-group / smooth-manifold structure on `UnitAddTorus` is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Existence of a maximal torus

anchor.snippet: in any compact Lie group G one can always find a maximal torus

annotations.note: No `MaximalTorus` / maximal-torus theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Protori

anchor.snippet: Toroidal groups are examples of protori

annotations.note: Protori / toroidal-groups infrastructure is absent.

annotations.label: Automorphisms of the torus from lattice automorphisms

anchor.snippet: Automorphisms of T are easily constructed from automorphisms of the lattice

annotations.note: Aut(T) ≃ GL(n,ℤ) via the lattice is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of the n-torus

anchor.snippet: The fundamental group of an n -torus is a free abelian group of rank n

annotations.note: π₁(Tⁿ) ≃ ℤⁿ is not stated; only the general product-isomorphism for groupoids exists.

annotations.label: k-th homology of the n-torus

anchor.snippet: The k th homology group of an n -torus is a free abelian group of rank n choose k

annotations.note: Hₖ(Tⁿ) ≃ ℤ^{n choose k} is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of the n-torus

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic of the n -torus is 0 for all n

annotations.note: χ(Tⁿ) = 0 is not stated; topological Euler-characteristic infrastructure for tori is absent.

annotations.label: Cohomology ring as exterior algebra

anchor.snippet: can be identified with the exterior algebra over the Z - module

annotations.note: `ExteriorAlgebra` exists but no identification with H*(Tⁿ;ℤ) is formalized.

annotations.label: n-torus as configuration space of ordered points on a circle

anchor.snippet: the n -torus is the configuration space of n ordered, not necessarily distinct points on the circle

annotations.note: Configuration-space interpretation of Tⁿ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unordered configuration space is the orbifold Tⁿ/Sₙ

anchor.snippet: The configuration space of unordered , not necessarily distinct points is accordingly the orbifold

annotations.note: Orbifold quotient Tⁿ/Sₙ is not formalized.

annotations.label: n = 2: quotient is the Möbius strip

anchor.snippet: For n = 2 , the quotient is the Möbius strip

annotations.note: Möbius strip is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: n = 3: quotient is a triangular-prism solid torus with a twist

anchor.snippet: For n = 3 this quotient may be described as a solid torus with cross-section an equilateral triangle

annotations.note: Specific Sym³(S¹) description is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat torus

anchor.snippet: A flat torus is a torus with the metric inherited from its representation as the quotient

annotations.note: `UnitAddTorus` carries the underlying quotient space, but it is not equipped with the flat Riemannian metric.

annotations.label: Square flat torus

anchor.snippet: This particular flat torus (and any uniformly scaled version of it) is known as the square flat torus

annotations.note: No named `SquareFlatTorus` / metric instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat torus has zero Gaussian curvature

anchor.snippet: Its surface has zero Gaussian curvature everywhere

annotations.note: Gaussian curvature is not formalized.

annotations.label: 4-dimensional Euclidean embedding of a rectangular flat torus

anchor.snippet: A simple 4-dimensional Euclidean embedding of a rectangular flat torus

annotations.note: Explicit ℝ⁴ embedding of the flat torus is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hopf coordinates on the 3-sphere

anchor.snippet: then u , v , and 0 < η < π/2 parameterize the unit 3-sphere as Hopf coordinates

annotations.note: Hopf-coordinate parametrisation of S³ is absent.

annotations.label: Flat torus partitions S³ into two congruent solid tori

anchor.snippet: the torus will partition the 3-sphere into two congruent solid tori subsets

annotations.note: Solid-torus decomposition of S³ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No C² embedding of a flat torus into 3-space

anchor.snippet: there exists no C 2 (twice continuously differentiable) embedding of a flat torus into 3-space

annotations.note: Non-embeddability of the flat torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Nash–Kuiper: existence of an isometric C¹ embedding

anchor.snippet: according to the Nash-Kuiper theorem , which was proven in the 1950s, an isometric C 1 embedding exists

annotations.note: No Nash–Kuiper theorem (grep finds nothing) in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Explicit C¹ isometric embedding (2012)

anchor.snippet: In April 2012, an explicit C 1 (continuously differentiable) isometric embedding of a flat torus into 3-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: Borrelli–Jabrane–Lazarus–Thibert construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conformally equivalent surfaces

anchor.snippet: two smooth compact geometric surfaces are "conformally equivalent" when there exists a smooth homeomorphism between them

annotations.note: Conformal-equivalence relation between surfaces is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: The Uniformization theorem guarantees that every Riemann surface is conformally equivalent to one that has constant Gaussian curvature

annotations.note: The Uniformization theorem is not in Mathlib (no `Uniformization` decl).

annotations.label: Moduli space M of flat tori as a punctured sphere

anchor.snippet: this moduli space M may be identified with a punctured sphere that is smooth except for two points

annotations.note: Moduli space M_{1,1} is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compactified moduli space M*

anchor.snippet: M may be turned into a compact space M* – topologically equivalent to a sphere

annotations.note: Compactified moduli space is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of M* via Gauss–Bonnet

anchor.snippet: the Gauss–Bonnet theorem shows that the area of each triangle can be calculated

annotations.note: Gauss–Bonnet theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square and hexagonal tori as the special conformal classes

anchor.snippet: The other two cusps occur at the points corresponding in M* to (a) the square torus

annotations.note: Special points of the moduli space are not formalized.

annotations.label: Genus g surface

anchor.snippet: A genus g surface is the connected sum of g two-tori

annotations.note: Connected sum of manifolds is not defined in Mathlib (grep finds only a SimpleGraph connectedSum).

annotations.label: Classification theorem for surfaces

anchor.snippet: The classification theorem for surfaces states that every compact connected surface is topologically equivalent to either the sphere or the connect sum

annotations.note: No surface classification theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Toroidal polyhedra

anchor.snippet: Polyhedra with the topological type of a torus are called toroidal polyhedra, and have Euler characteristic

annotations.note: Toroidal polyhedra are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic V − E + F = 2 − 2g

anchor.snippet: For any number of holes, the formula generalizes to

annotations.note: Polyhedral Euler-characteristic with genus correction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Seven colors suffice for maps on the torus

anchor.snippet: Toroidal polyhedra have been used to show that the maximum number of colors to color a map on a torus is seven

annotations.note: Seven-colour theorem on the torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Szilassi polyhedron

anchor.snippet: The Szilassi polyhedron is one example of a toroidal polyhedron with this property

annotations.note: Szilassi polyhedron not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Császár polyhedron has every vertex-pair as an edge

anchor.snippet: The Szilassi polyhedron's dual, the Császár polyhedron

annotations.note: Császár polyhedron not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mapping class group surjects onto GL(2, ℤ)

anchor.snippet: Its mapping class group (the connected components of the homeomorphism group) is surjective onto the group

annotations.note: Mapping class group of the torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mapping class group short exact sequence splits

anchor.snippet: Thus the short exact sequence of the mapping class group splits

annotations.note: Splitting of the MCG sequence is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heawood number of the torus is seven

anchor.snippet: The torus's Heawood number is seven, meaning every graph that can be embedded on the torus has a chromatic number of at most seven

annotations.note: Heawood numbers and graph genus-embeddability are not formalized.

annotations.label: de Bruijn torus

anchor.snippet: a de Bruijn torus is an array of symbols from an alphabet

annotations.note: No `deBruijnTorus` in Mathlib (only de Bruijn indices unrelated to combinatorics).

annotations.label: Maximum number of pieces from cutting a solid torus

anchor.snippet: A solid torus of revolution can be cut by n (> 0) planes into at most

annotations.note: Cake-number formula for the torus is not formalized.

annotations.label: First 11 values of the cutting sequence

anchor.snippet: The first 11 numbers of parts, for 0 ≤ n ≤ 10

annotations.note: Tabulated values for the cutting sequence are not formalized.
